1.title: Chapter 1: Relative value and cash-and-carry
1.learning_objective: Derive fair value via replication, measure residuals, and map arbitrage direction under explicit frictions.
1.summary: Introduces law-of-one-price intuition, payoff replication, friction-adjusted fair value, basis/residual interpretation, and directional cash-and-carry decisions.
quotes: Basis sign is a decision rule only after financing is modeled.
Execution discipline turns theoretical edge into realizable P&L.
derivation_steps: Replication: construct synthetic futures using spot plus financing and matched maturity payoff.
Fair price: solve no-arbitrage fair value after adding friction layers.
Residual: compute market minus fair value and map sign to direction and confidence.

failure_modes.mitigation: Apply transaction costs, repo stress, and balance-sheet costs before trading.

2.title: Chapter 2: Mean reversion with Ornstein-Uhlenbeck dynamics
2.learning_objective: Quantify spread reversion and first-passage risk.
2.summary: Models spread evolution as an OU process so half-life, barrier-hit probability, and terminal risk-adjusted payoff can be estimated for holding-period decisions.
quotes: Half-life is useful only relative to your holding horizon.
First-passage risk is a path property, not a terminal-value property.
derivation_steps: Calibrate OU parameters from spread history.
Simulate discrete paths and evaluate first-passage to target barrier.
Aggregate hit probability, mean first-passage time, and terminal Sharpe diagnostics.

failure_modes.mode: Treating regime shifts as stationary noise
failure_modes.mitigation: Use rolling calibration and structural break checks.

3.title: Chapter 3: PCA factor extraction for the rates curve
3.learning_objective: Extract orthogonal curve factors for diagnostics.
3.summary: Uses covariance-eigen decomposition to produce factor scores through time, reconstruction controls, residualized series, and candidate factor-neutral hedge weights.
quotes: Explained variance is a ranking tool, not an economic guarantee.
Stable factors require stable data engineering.

equations.name: Partial reconstruction with first k PCs

derivation_steps: Standardize input panel into Z-scores matrix Z.
Compute covariance matrix, then sort eigenpairs descending by eigenvalue.
Compute factor scores through time F = Z @ eigenvectors.
Enable selectable reconstruction using first k components (PC1 only, PC1+PC2, etc.).
Compute residualized panel after removing selected PCs and construct candidate neutral hedge weights.
Interpret leading factors as likely level/slope/curvature with explicit caveat that PCA factors are statistical.

failure_modes.mitigation: Use changes/returns and monitor loading drift.

4.title: Chapter 4: Factor interpretation and regime mapping
4.learning_objective: Translate statistical factors into market narratives and regime tags.
4.summary: Maps statistical PCA factors into economic regime labels, producing a composite regime score and confidence used to gate relative-value signal aggressiveness.
quotes: A factor is useful only when you can name its risk.
Interpretability is a risk-control requirement, not a reporting feature.
derivation_steps: Name major factors economically (level/slope/curvature).
Combine standardized factors into a weighted regime score.
Map score bands to labels and convert distance-from-threshold into confidence.

failure_modes.mitigation: Use out-of-sample validation and robust threshold bands.

5.title: Chapter 5: Duration-convexity fair-value diagnostics
5.learning_objective: Translate curve shocks into price response.
5.summary: Uses duration and convexity approximations to translate yield-curve shocks into bond price responses and shock-adjusted fair-value estimates.
quotes: Duration is linear memory; convexity is nonlinear correction.
Approximation error grows faster than most traders expect.
derivation_steps: Select scenario shock dy in basis points and convert to decimal yield change.
Apply first- and second-order terms to estimate percent price move.
Convert estimated move into shock-adjusted fair price and slope diagnostic.

failure_modes.mode: Using local approximation for large shocks
failure_modes.mitigation: Reprice full cashflows for stressed scenarios.

6.title: Chapter 6: Multi-curve construction and basis diagnostics
6.learning_objective: Build consistent discount and projection curves for valuation.
6.summary: Builds collateral-consistent discount factors and tenor projection forwards, then measures IBOR-OIS basis to ensure valuation consistency across instruments.
quotes: Curve choice is a model decision with P&L consequences.
No-arbitrage requires curve consistency across instruments.
derivation_steps: Bootstrap OIS discount factors from liquid collateralized instruments.
Construct tenor-specific projection rates from matching accrual conventions.
Compare projection and discount curves to compute basis and residual consistency checks.

failure_modes.mitigation: Standardize day-count, calendars, and collateral assumptions.

7.title: Chapter 7: Bond Futures Contracts
7.learning_objective: Price and hedge deliverable bond futures under CTD optionality and repo-driven carry.
7.summary: Introduces futures invoice mechanics, conversion factors, cheapest-to-deliver (CTD) selection, implied repo, and hedge-ratio construction linking cash bonds to listed futures.
quotes: CTD switches are option events, not bookkeeping noise.
A futures hedge is only as good as its delivery assumptions.
derivation_steps: Compute invoice value for each deliverable bond using futures price, conversion factor, and projected accrued interest at delivery.
Carry each candidate through delivery date with repo financing and coupons to estimate implied repo and net basis.
Select CTD as the bond with strongest delivery economics and derive DV01-neutral hedge ratio against the cash position.

failure_modes.mode: Ignoring CTD switch risk near delivery
failure_modes.mitigation: Run scenario analysis across yield-curve shocks and monitor delivery-option boundary daily.
Refresh specialness and term repo curves intraday for stressed collateral.

prerequisites: Chapter 6 discount factors and forward rates

8.title: Chapter 8: Fitted Bond Curves
8.learning_objective: Fit arbitrage-aware government bond curves and extract robust rich/cheap diagnostics.
8.summary: Builds parametric and spline-based zero curves from benchmark bonds, then translates residuals into standardized rich/cheap measures conditioned on liquidity and benchmark effects.
quotes: A curve fit is a model of market microstructure as much as term structure.
Residuals without diagnostics are just interpolation errors.
derivation_steps: Calibrate curve parameters to minimize weighted pricing errors across liquid benchmark bonds.
Convert fitted zero yields into discount factors and reprice each bond for residual diagnostics.
Standardize residuals by maturity-bucket uncertainty and rank rich/cheap candidates for follow-on analytics.

failure_modes.mitigation: Apply smoothness penalties and out-of-sample residual validation by tenor bucket.

failure_modes.mode: Mixing illiquid prints with benchmark prices
failure_modes.mitigation: Use liquidity-weighted objective functions and stale-quote filtering.

9.title: Chapter 9: An Analytic Process for Government Bond Markets
9.learning_objective: Assemble a repeatable top-down and cross-sectional workflow for government bond RV decisions.
9.summary: Defines a full analytic pipeline from macro regime framing to curve-relative valuation, relative-value trade candidate selection, and governance gates for government bond markets.
quotes: Process quality compounds faster than isolated trade ideas.
Government RV requires linking macro narrative to instrument-level math.
derivation_steps: Classify macro regime and policy-path priors to set directional and volatility context.
Map fitted-curve residuals and carry/rolldown metrics into tenor and structure-level relative-value signals.
Aggregate standardized signals into a governed scorecard with explicit entry, sizing, and monitoring criteria.

failure_modes.mitigation: Use probabilistic regime weights and scenario-robust signal aggregation.
Orthogonalize factor blocks before final composite scoring.

10.title: Chapter 10: Overview of Mutual Influences in Rates Markets
10.learning_objective: Map swap-block dependencies and feedback loops across sovereign, swap, funding, and credit-linked rates markets.
10.summary: Builds a dependency map showing how government curves, swap spreads, basis, collateral funding, and credit premia co-move, and identifies channels through which shocks propagate.
quotes: Swap blocks are coupled systems, not isolated valuation modules.
Dependency maps turn correlation anecdotes into causal hypotheses.
derivation_steps: Define state vector of key rates blocks (government, swap, basis, funding, credit) and estimate dependency matrix.
Impose sign/economic constraints to separate structural influences from coincident noise.
Run shock-propagation scenarios to quantify first- and second-round impacts on valuation and hedge blocks.

failure_modes.mitigation: Use lagged identification checks and economic sign restrictions.
Augment linear map with regime-specific overrides for funding stress episodes.

11.title: Chapter 11: Reference Rates
11.learning_objective: Compare and operationalize overnight and term reference rates for pricing, accrual, and risk transfer.
11.summary: Covers construction and usage of SOFR, €STR, SONIA, and term benchmarks; links compounding conventions to coupon cashflows, discounting, and basis risk.
quotes: Reference rate choice is an exposure choice.
Compounding conventions are valuation parameters, not clerical detail.
derivation_steps: Build compounded-in-arrears index from daily overnight fixings and accrual fractions.
Translate contractual conventions (lookback, lockout, observation shift) into effective coupon settings.
Compare term and compounded benchmarks to isolate basis risk and hedge implications.

failure_modes.mitigation: Implement convention-specific calculators with unit tests for lookback/lockout variants.

failure_modes.mode: Assuming fallback spread is static in risk systems
failure_modes.mitigation: Model fallback and basis as explicit stochastic risk factors.

12.title: Chapter 12: Asset Swaps
12.learning_objective: Price, decompose, and risk-manage asset-swap packages across credit, curve, and funding dimensions.
12.summary: Builds full asset-swap package valuation from bond cashflows and swap legs, decomposes package spread drivers, and links outputs to executable hedging and monitoring.
quotes: Asset swaps are balance-sheet trades wearing valuation equations.
Package spread without funding context is incomplete risk information.
derivation_steps: Discount bond and swap-leg cashflows on consistent collateral curves to solve par package spread.
Linearize package sensitivities across rates, credit spread, and funding factors for risk attribution.
Convert spread and carry decomposition into entry thresholds, hedge ratios, and monitoring triggers.

failure_modes.mode: Curve inconsistency between bond and swap legs
failure_modes.mitigation: Use aligned CSA-aware discount/projection setup and reconciliation checks.
Stress repo term structure and specialness scenarios in package valuation.

13.title: Chapter 13: Pure-credit extraction
13.learning_objective: Isolate default-risk premium by stripping liquidity and technical basis effects.
13.summary: Separates observed spread into default and non-default components and maps pure-credit spread into hazard-rate proxies.
quotes: Pure credit is a residual, so component hygiene matters.
Recovery assumptions are leverage points in hazard inference.
derivation_steps: Begin with all-in observed spread and align measurement windows across bond, CDS, and liquidity proxies.
Estimate and subtract non-default components (liquidity and technical basis) to isolate pure credit residual.
Translate residual spread into implied hazard using an explicit recovery assumption and report sensitivity to recovery grid.

failure_modes.mitigation: Cross-check proxy set (bid-ask, depth, fails) and run orthogonality diagnostics.
Publish scenario band for recovery and require robustness of signal sign.

13.core_claim: Observed credit spread must be decomposed into default and non-default components before hazard inferences are tradable.
step_by_step_derivation: Begin with all-in observed spread and align measurement windows across bond, CDS, and liquidity proxies.
Estimate and subtract non-default components (liquidity and technical basis) to isolate pure credit residual.
Translate residual spread into implied hazard using an explicit recovery assumption and report sensitivity to recovery grid.
trade_interpretation: Long pure-credit cheapness when residual spread is wide versus peer hazard-implied fair value.
Size conviction down when hazard estimate is highly sensitive to recovery assumptions.

failure_modes_model_risk.mitigation: Cross-check proxy set (bid-ask, depth, fails) and run orthogonality diagnostics.
Publish scenario band for recovery and require robustness of signal sign.

checkpoint_assessment.question: Which input most directly changes the hazard denominator?
checkpoint_assessment.validation: Learner explains why (1-recovery) scales implied default intensity.

exports.handoff: Used as credit-clean input for tenor and cross-currency basis packaging.

14.title: Chapter 14: Intra-currency basis swaps
14.learning_objective: Measure and trade tenor-basis dislocations within one currency curve while accounting for funding frictions.
14.summary: Applies cost-aware thresholds and confidence scaling to decide whether a modeled opportunity should be released for execution.
quotes: Execution confidence should shrink faster than edge in stressed liquidity.
Thresholds are governance tools, not optimization artifacts.
derivation_steps: Construct fair basis from OIS discounting, projection curves, and collateral convention.
Compute observed-minus-fair dislocation and subtract cost stack (impact, funding, balance-sheet usage).
Convert residual edge into execution confidence and binary release flag via governance threshold.

failure_modes.mitigation: Calibrate costs by size/venue and include stressed liquidity add-ons.
Enforce discount/projection convention checks before signal publication.

14.core_claim: Intra-currency tenor basis should be traded only when fair-value dislocation exceeds fully loaded implementation and funding frictions.
step_by_step_derivation: Construct fair basis from OIS discounting, projection curves, and collateral convention.
Compute observed-minus-fair dislocation and subtract cost stack (impact, funding, balance-sheet usage).
Convert residual edge into execution confidence and binary release flag via governance threshold.
trade_interpretation: Positive net edge with high confidence supports receiving expensive tenor leg / paying cheap leg package.
Near-threshold signals should remain watchlist ideas instead of immediate execution.

failure_modes_model_risk.mitigation: Calibrate costs by size/venue and include stressed liquidity add-ons.
Enforce discount/projection convention checks before signal publication.

checkpoint_assessment.question: Why can gross basis dislocation still be non-tradable?
checkpoint_assessment.answer: Costs and hurdle can absorb the edge.
checkpoint_assessment.validation: Learner computes basis_edge_bp and compares to entry_hurdle_bp.

exports.handoff: Feeds cross-currency package allocator and expected-return budgeting.

15.title: Chapter 15: Cross-currency basis swaps
15.learning_objective: Translate cross-currency basis dislocations into expected carry-plus-convergence P&L for hedge-funded packages.
15.summary: Builds forward return estimates from carry and rolldown, net of implementation costs and horizon assumptions.
quotes: Carry is time income only if position survives path risk.
Budgeting errors compound when horizons are misaligned.
derivation_steps: Convert quoted basis into annualized carry under collateral/funding convention.
Project convergence component from current basis gap and horizon mean-reversion assumption.
Subtract hedge/funding costs to obtain expected_total_pnl and decompose contribution shares.

failure_modes.mitigation: Align carry accrual and convergence assumptions with actual holding-period mandate.
Use regime-conditioned convergence speeds and scenario envelopes.

15.core_claim: Cross-currency basis value is realized through carry plus convergence, net of hedge and funding frictions over a defined horizon.
step_by_step_derivation: Convert quoted basis into annualized carry under collateral/funding convention.
Project convergence component from current basis gap and horizon mean-reversion assumption.
Subtract hedge/funding costs to obtain expected_total_pnl and decompose contribution shares.
trade_interpretation: Prefer packages where expected_total_pnl remains positive under conservative funding scenarios.
Carry-heavy trades with weak convergence should be capped by path-risk limits.

failure_modes_model_risk.mitigation: Align carry accrual and convergence assumptions with actual holding-period mandate.
Use regime-conditioned convergence speeds and scenario envelopes.

checkpoint_assessment.question: Which component captures mark-to-fair normalization?
checkpoint_assessment.validation: Learner separates carry income from convergence effect in attribution table.

exports.handoff: Inputs integrated hedge sizing for asset-basis-plus-CDS package.

16.title: Chapter 16: Integrated asset-basis and CDS
16.learning_objective: Jointly size cash-bond, basis-swap, and CDS legs to preserve target carry while controlling residual rate risk.
16.summary: Calibrates hedge ratios using DV01 targets and residual risk constraints for portfolio-consistent risk control.
quotes: Perfect hedges are rare; controlled residuals are the goal.
Sensitivity freshness matters as much as sensitivity precision.
derivation_steps: Aggregate DV01 for cash asset and basis legs after Chapter 15 carry/convergence selection.
Solve CDS notional (or units) to hit target residual DV01 consistent with macro risk budget.
Validate realized residual and stress stability before exporting hedge ratio to global RV ranking.

failure_modes.mitigation: Track rolling hedge beta and require rebalance triggers.
Refresh DV01/CS01 on latest curves and spread levels before execution.

16.core_claim: Integrated asset-basis and CDS positions should target desired carry while neutralizing unwanted rate exposure via explicit residual DV01 constraints.
step_by_step_derivation: Aggregate DV01 for cash asset and basis legs after Chapter 15 carry/convergence selection.
Solve CDS notional (or units) to hit target residual DV01 consistent with macro risk budget.
Validate realized residual and stress stability before exporting hedge ratio to global RV ranking.
trade_interpretation: Higher hedge_ratio_units indicates more CDS required to keep package macro-rate neutral.
If realized residual DV01 drifts from target, rebalance or reduce gross exposure.

failure_modes_model_risk.mitigation: Track rolling hedge beta and require rebalance triggers.
Refresh DV01/CS01 on latest curves and spread levels before execution.

checkpoint_assessment.question: What confirms hedge calibration success?
checkpoint_assessment.validation: Learner recomputes residual after applying solved hedge units.

exports.handoff: Consumed by global RV stress P&L engine.

17.title: Chapter 17: Global bond RV
17.learning_objective: Rank relative-value bond packages across regions using hedge-adjusted spread carry and stress-aware downside filters.
17.summary: Computes stressed P&L envelopes from spread/rate sensitivities and liquidity penalties for downside governance.
quotes: Stress testing is about joint moves, not single shocks.
Liquidity penalties belong in risk, not post-mortems.
derivation_steps: Apply correlated global spread and rate shock vectors to each hedge-adjusted RV package.
Compute drawdown using CS01 and residual DV01, then aggregate to scenario stress P&L.
Deduct liquidity haircut to produce total_stress_pnl used for capital-governed ranking.

failure_modes.mitigation: Use correlated multi-factor and region-specific stress templates.
Link haircut to market depth and volatility regime indicators.

17.core_claim: Global RV ranking must compare hedge-adjusted carry opportunities under joint spread-rate stress and explicit liquidity penalties.
step_by_step_derivation: Apply correlated global spread and rate shock vectors to each hedge-adjusted RV package.
Compute drawdown using CS01 and residual DV01, then aggregate to scenario stress P&L.
Deduct liquidity haircut to produce total_stress_pnl used for capital-governed ranking.
trade_interpretation: Prefer packages with superior carry per unit of stressed downside after liquidity penalties.
High gross carry can still rank low if stress losses dominate expected return.

failure_modes_model_risk.mitigation: Use correlated multi-factor and region-specific stress templates.
Link haircut to market depth and volatility regime indicators.

checkpoint_assessment.question: Why subtract liquidity_haircut after CS01/DV01 stress?
checkpoint_assessment.answer: To include executable downside, not just theoretical mark-to-market.
checkpoint_assessment.validation: Learner compares rankings with and without haircut adjustment.

exports.handoff: Passed into Chapter 18 capital shadow-cost gate.

18.title: Chapter 18: Capital shadow costs
18.learning_objective: Price capital usage and funding frictions into final trade acceptance decisions.
18.summary: Final governance layer that nets expected alpha against capital and funding charges to determine approval readiness.
quotes: Positive gross alpha can still fail economic value tests.
Capital is a scarce input, not a free buffer.
derivation_steps: Import expected_pnl and stress diagnostics from prior chapters for candidate packages.
Compute capital and funding shadow costs under current transfer-pricing and balance-sheet policy.
Approve trade only if risk_adjusted_alpha remains positive and policy thresholds are met.

failure_modes.mitigation: Synchronize with treasury transfer-pricing updates and policy calendar.
Run marginal and incremental capital simulations by position size.

18.core_claim: Final trade approval requires expected alpha to clear capital and funding shadow costs, not just positive gross P&L.
step_by_step_derivation: Import expected_pnl and stress diagnostics from prior chapters for candidate packages.
Compute capital and funding shadow costs under current transfer-pricing and balance-sheet policy.
Approve trade only if risk_adjusted_alpha remains positive and policy thresholds are met.
trade_interpretation: Positive risk_adjusted_alpha supports deployment; negative values imply capital is better allocated elsewhere.
Borderline approvals should include explicit remediation plan (size reduction or cheaper funding path).

failure_modes_model_risk.mitigation: Synchronize with treasury transfer-pricing updates and policy calendar.
Run marginal and incremental capital simulations by position size.

checkpoint_assessment.question: What is the final go/no-go metric?
checkpoint_assessment.validation: Learner calculates capital_charge then risk_adjusted_alpha from supplied inputs.

exports.handoff: Terminal governance output for portfolio allocation committee.
